recipe.title: Spaghetti alla Carbonara
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the spaghetti until al dente.
Meanwhile, dice the guanciale and render it in a cold pan over medium heat until crisp.
In a bowl, whisk the yolks, whole egg, Pecorino, and a generous amount of black pepper.
Drain the pasta, reserving a cup of pasta water, and add the spaghetti to the pan with the guanciale (off the heat).
Pour the egg mixture over the pasta, tossing quickly and adding splashes of pasta water until silky.
Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and pepper on top.

recipe.title: Chicken Biryani
ingredients: 1/2 tsp saffron soaked in 50 ml warm milk
1 tsp wheat flour (for sealing the lid)
steps: Marinate the chicken in yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, turmeric, and salt for at least 1 hour.
Fry the onions in ghee until deep golden, then reserve half for layering.
Parboil the basmati rice in heavily salted water with whole spices until 70% cooked, then drain.
Layer rice over the chicken in a heavy pot, top with fried onions, saffron milk, and chopped herbs.
Seal the lid with a wheat-flour dough rope and cook on dum (low heat) for 25 minutes.
Rest for 10 minutes before opening and gently fluffing the rice.

recipe.title: Classic Fudgy Brownies
steps: Preheat the oven to 175 °C and line a 23 cm square tin with parchment.
Melt the butter and stir in the sugar, then let cool for 2 minutes.
Whisk in the eggs and vanilla, followed by the cocoa, flour, and salt.
Fold in the walnuts if using and spread the batter into the tin.
Bake for 25-28 minutes until the top is set and the centre is still slightly soft.
Cool completely in the tin before slicing into 12 squares.
